category_title: At-risk special education students who struggle to visualize historical narratives and connect emotionally to printed text
scenario_title: Comprehending and visualizing historical printed text
minimal_context: The student is answering homework questions about the opening chapter of the novel Night during a class discussion.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We just finished reading the opening chapter of Night aloud. Let's look at the homework questions. Can you describe why Moishe the Beadle was an important character?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Remains silent and apprehensive) I just tried to get through the reading as quickly as possible.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think about what happened in the chapter?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I felt confused by it at first. Why didn't any of the people stick up for themselves?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's focus on the question about Moishe the Beadle. What makes him important to the story?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't know why he's important. I wasn't really sure what the gas chambers and the cattle cars looked like just from reading. It was hard to picture all of it and what it looked like.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We just finished reading the opening chapter of Night aloud. Let's look at the homework questions. Can you describe why Moishe the Beadle was an important character?

STUDENT: (Remains silent and apprehensive) I just tried to get through the reading as quickly as possible.

TUTOR: What do you think about what happened in the chapter?

STUDENT: I felt confused by it at first. Why didn't any of the people stick up for themselves?

TUTOR: Let's focus on the question about Moishe the Beadle. What makes him important to the story?

STUDENT: I don't know why he's important. I wasn't really sure what the gas chambers and the cattle cars looked like just from reading. It was hard to picture all of it and what it looked like.

task.content: Completing independent reading assignments, homework questions, and quizzes based on chapters of the novel Night by Elie Wiesel, and identifying specific nuances or details, such as why Moishe the Beadle was an important character.
task.learning_goal: Appreciate texts as literary works of art through engaging in aesthetic reading that connects the reader to the text both personally and emotionally.

context.materials: Elie Wiesel’s (1960) autobiographical novel Night, blackboard, Cinego digital movie projector, sheet of paper with quotes from Night
context.social_setting: Class discussion and small group discussion
context.time_context: First half of the school year

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the teacher paused now and again to ask questions after reading aloud the opening chapter, despite the author’s vivid descriptions.
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Homework answers and quizzes based on the novel contained answers that were incorrect and vague. Could not describe why Moishe the Beadle was an important character.
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Confused and had trouble understanding the gravity of the subject material.
related_quotes: I wasn’t really sure what the gas chambers and the cattle cars looked like just from reading. It was hard to picture all of it and what it looked like but it was easier after watching the film.

behavior_patterns.condition: When asked to respond to each other collaboratively about the topic.
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Exhibited silent, apprehensive and ambiguous responses to each other collaboratively.
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Uncertain about the topic and disconnected.
related_quotes: I felt confused by the poem at first and then we reread it and I understood it a little better.

behavior_patterns.condition: When assigned reading materials as efferent readers.
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Attention is focused primarily on extracting information: getting through the reading as quickly as possible for practical purposes.
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Much confusion about why Jews were targeted, hated and segregated.
related_quotes: Why didn’t any of the people stick up for themselves?
